task_completion.description: Open and read the storage compatibility query file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/storage_compatibility_query.txt, showing the list of chemicals in Room 215.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/.

task_completion.description: Add Hydrogen Peroxide (>60%) and the queried chemicals to the CAMEO list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals interface showing 'Hydrogen Peroxide' (UN 2015 / CAS 7722-84-1) and other chemicals from the input list added to the 'MyChemicals' list.

task_completion.description: Run the compatibility assessment in CAMEO Chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals compatibility prediction chart or hazard matrix displayed in the browser, showing the interactions between Hydrogen Peroxide and the other listed chemicals.

task_completion.description: Save the comprehensive compatibility report with all required details
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the file saved at ~/Documents/h2o2_compatibility_report.txt. The content must visibly include H2O2's reactive groups, incompatible reactive groups, specific chemical matches from Room 215, and storage recommendations.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a real web browser to access CAMEO Chemicals
integrity.visual_evidence: A graphical web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) should be visible navigating the CAMEO website, not just terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the CAMEO compatibility tool
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the CAMEO Chemicals compatibility results page/matrix being loaded, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate the chemical interactions.

integrity.description: Verify the report is saved in the correct directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor's title bar, save dialog, or a terminal `ls` command should confirm the file is specifically named 'h2o2_compatibility_report.txt' and located in the '~/Documents/' directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Hydrogen Peroxide (>60%) has CAS number 7722-84-1 and UN number 2015. In CAMEO Chemicals, its reactive groups are 'Oxidizing Agents, Strong' and 'Water and Aqueous Solutions'. The final report must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/h2o2_compatibility_report.txt and must explicitly include these reactive groups, the incompatible reactive groups identified by CAMEO, specific hazardous matches from the Room 215 list, and storage recommendations.
